events.title: Mother's Day Brunch
events.description: Mother's Day Brunch on Lavelle Road — Bengaluru's most beloved dining stretch comes alive with families celebrating the most important women in their lives over beautiful food, flowers, and the kind of warmth that only a Sunday with your mother can bring.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Mother's Day Brunch on Lavelle Road is a premium family dining event — genZScore 4-7. The crowd spans two distinct dressing modes simultaneously: (1) the mothers being celebrated — elevated, graceful, and beautifully dressed for a special occasion; (2) the GenZ children taking their mothers out — smart casual, put-together, and clearly making an effort. Lavelle Road context is key — this is Bengaluru's premium dining corridor, which elevates the dressing slightly above a casual brunch. The fashion energy is warm, feminine, and celebratory without being overly formal. Floral prints are the single most relevant pattern — Mother's Day and florals are inseparable. Colors are soft and warm — blush, dusty rose, lavender, sage, cream, soft yellow, mint. May in Bengaluru means warmth — breathable fabrics, lighter silhouettes, and summer-appropriate dressing. Women in floral midi dresses, elegant co-ords, or a soft printed ethnic set. Men in a clean pastel or neutral shirt with smart trousers. Jewellery is elegant and gifted — pearl pieces, dainty gold, and meaningful accessories are particularly relevant today.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Mother's Day Brunch on Lavelle Road is the warmest and most intentional dressing moment of May — two generations making an effort for each other. Floral prints are the absolute hero pattern of the day — a floral midi dress in soft pastels is the most catalog-relevant piece for this event. Women in a blush or dusty rose floral midi dress, a soft sage linen co-ord with delicate jewellery, or an elegant ethnic set in pastel tones for mothers who prefer traditional dressing. A chikankari kurti in white or cream is a beautiful ethnic option for mothers. Men in a clean pastel shirt — soft pink, sage, or cream — with well-fitted light trousers — making an effort without overdressing. Pearl jewellery is uniquely iconic for Mother's Day — pearl necklaces, pearl earrings, and pearl-detail pieces are the most gifted and worn accessories of the day. Dainty gold pieces complement the soft palette beautifully. Small structured bags or soft totes in pastel or nude tones. The May Bengaluru warmth calls for breathable fabrics — linen, cotton, and rayon over heavier materials. The overall energy is — dressed like you want her to feel celebrated, whether you're the mother or the child.

app.headline: Brunch With Mum. Dress With Love.
lines: Florals, pearls, and soft pastels — Mother's Day sorted.
Lavelle Road brunch calls for your most thoughtful fit.
Dress like she deserves the most beautiful table in the room.

social_media.headline: Mum Deserves The Floral Dress Era.
lines: POV: took mum to Lavelle Road brunch and her dusty rose floral midi dress became the whole afternoon.
Mother's Day fit check — soft florals, pearl jewels, pastel co-ords, dressed with love for the most important brunch of the year.

events.title: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu
events.genres: Science Fiction, Action, Adventure, Space Opera
events.description: Din Djarin and Grogu return to the big screen — as the fledgling New Republic battles the remnants of a scattered Empire, the galaxy's most beloved bounty hunter and his young apprentice navigate a universe where the old ways and new beginnings collide in the most heartfelt Star Wars story ever told.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: The Mandalorian and Grogu is one of the most culturally beloved Star Wars properties — genZScore 7-9. The Mandalorian fan base is emotionally invested, aesthetically aware, and spans generations — from original Star Wars fans to new GenZ audiences. The fashion energy is uniquely specific to Din Djarin's world — dark tactical, space western, Mandalorian beskar armor aesthetics, and the warm emotional undertone of a found-family story. The screening crowd dresses with deep affection — Mandalorian-coded fashion is the brief. The color palette from the poster — cool grey, deep charcoal, and dark near-black — perfectly captures the Mandalorian's dark metallic armor language. Three distinct styling directions: (1) Mandalorian armor-inspired — dark grey, charcoal, slate, and metallic silver with tactical details; (2) space western — dark earth tones, cargo details, and the frontier aesthetic of the outer rim; (3) Grogu soft energy — the most beloved character in Star Wars history opens a soft, warm, adorable fashion counter-direction. May in Bengaluru is intense heat — lightweight breathable fabrics are essential for the screening crowd. The dark metallic palette works beautifully even in summer when executed in lightweight polyester and cotton.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: The Mandalorian and Grogu screening is the most emotionally warm tactical dressing moment of May — This Is The Way applies to outfit choices too. Lead with the Mandalorian's dark beskar palette — charcoal grey, slate, and silver metallic in lightweight summer-appropriate fabrics. A charcoal grey co-ord in polyester or microfiber, a dark tactical-inspired outfit with cargo details, or a clean all-black set with silver accessories captures the Mandalorian armor aesthetic without the actual armor. Women in a dark grey or charcoal co-ord with silver jewellery, or a slate-toned dress with metallic accessories — cool, considered, and perfectly Mandalorian-coded. Men in a dark charcoal or slate grey tactical-inspired outfit — cargo trousers with a clean fitted dark tee and a structured dark jacket. The space western direction opens earth-toned options — brown leather-look jacket over a cream tee with dark cargo trousers references the outer rim frontier aesthetic. May Bengaluru heat means lightweight polyester and microfiber for the dark palette — both breathable and fast-drying for the monsoon transition. The Grogu counter-direction is equally valid and catalog-relevant — soft green and warm cream pieces with character-adjacent cute accessories for the fans who dress for the heart of the story rather than the armor. Caps in dark tones. Chain jewellery in silver. The overall energy is — This Is The Way, and the way is a very well-considered dark outfit.

app.headline: This Is The Way To Dress Tonight.
lines: Charcoal, slate, and silver — the Mandalorian palette needs no explanation.
Dark tactical or soft Grogu energy — both are valid screening choices.
The galaxy's best bounty hunter has excellent taste in dark greys.

social_media.headline: Grogu Approves. The Outfit Does Not Miss.
lines: Mandalorian screening dress code — dark charcoal, cargo details, silver accessories. This is the way.
Din Djarin never takes off his helmet but you can take off in this charcoal co-ord. The Mandalorian and Grogu hits screens May 22nd. 🪖✨

events.title: Bakrid (Eid al-Adha)
events.description: Bakrid — Eid al-Adha — is the festival of sacrifice, marking Ibrahim's devotion to Allah with communal prayers, family feasts, the sharing of meat with those in need, and the most dressed-up gathering of the Islamic calendar after Eid-ul-Fitr.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Bakrid is one of the two most important festivals in Islam — genZScore 4-6. The dressing energy is deeply similar to Eid-ul-Fitr but with its own distinct character — slightly more formal and traditional, as Bakrid is considered the more sacred of the two Eids. The crowd spans all ages with a strong family and community focus. Morning Eid prayers demand the most traditional and modest dressing — white and off-white are the most auspicious prayer colors for men. Post-prayer family gatherings allow more color and celebration. May in Bengaluru means significant heat — breathable fabrics are essential. Women in anarkalis, sharara sets, salwar suits, or richly embroidered ethnic dresses in rich but modest tones. Men in sharply tailored sherwanis, kurta-pyjamas, or pathani suits. The color palette for Bakrid leans slightly richer and deeper than Eid-ul-Fitr pastels — deep greens, rich blues, burgundy, ivory, and gold are strongly associated with this festival. Jewellery is traditional and rich — gold-tone, kundan, and polki-inspired pieces.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Bakrid dressing carries a deeper gravitas than Eid-ul-Fitr — the colors are richer, the dressing more formal, and the intention more profound. Deep green is the most sacred and iconic Bakrid color — it represents Islam's most revered color and is uniquely dominant on this day. Women in a deeply embroidered anarkali in forest green or deep burgundy, a richly embellished sharara set in ivory and gold, or a zardosi-detailed ethnic dress in navy or royal blue — floor-length and fully covered but deeply festive. Chikankari and mukaish embroidery traditions are uniquely relevant — delicate white-on-white or green embroidery on georgette or silk-feel fabrics. Men in a crisp white kurta-pyjama for morning prayers — the most auspicious and traditional Bakrid prayer look — then a sharply tailored sherwani or pathani suit in deep green, navy, or burgundy for post-prayer celebrations. May Bengaluru heat makes breathable georgette-feel and cotton-blend fabrics essential over heavy silks. Gold-tone jewellery is the Bakrid signature — kundan and polki-inspired necklaces, jhumkas, bangles, and maang tikka. Pearl detail accessories are very relevant. Kids ethnic sets and sherwanis in deep green are the most heartwarming catalog category. The overall energy is — dressed with deep faith, family pride, and the richness that this sacred festival deserves.

app.headline: Bakrid Mubarak. Dress With Faith.
lines: Deep green, ivory, and gold — the sacred Bakrid palette.
Sherwani for prayers, anarkali for the feast — both sorted.
Dressed for the most sacred gathering of the year.

social_media.headline: Bakrid Fits That Honor The Day.
lines: POV: showed up to Bakrid in a forest green zardosi anarkali and the whole family gathered closer.
Bakrid fit check — deep green ethnic, gold jewellery, floor-length grace. Eid Mubarak.

events.title: UEFA Champions League Final
events.genres: Sports, Football, International, Screening, Fan Culture
events.description: The UEFA Champions League Final — the biggest club football match on the planet — screened live across Bengaluru's sports bars, fan parks, and viewing parties as the world holds its breath for 90 minutes of football history.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: UEFA Champions League Final is a massive global sports moment — genZScore 7-9. Football culture in India has grown enormously — the UCL Final draws passionate fan communities across Bengaluru's sports bars and screening venues. The fashion energy is significantly different from cricket watch parties — football fan culture has its own distinct aesthetic. Three distinct crowd contexts: (1) hardcore football fans — club jerseys, scarves, team colors, full fan kit; (2) casual football enthusiasts — smart casual with team color accents; (3) sports bar social crowd — trendy casual with football-adjacent styling. Club jersey culture is the hero — Real Madrid white, Barcelona blue and red, Manchester City sky blue, Bayern Munich red, PSG blue. But UCL Final also attracts neutral fans who dress in the iconic UCL branding colors — navy, gold, and star motifs. The GenZ football crowd styles jerseys as fashion pieces — oversized, layered, or tucked. May Bengaluru heat means lightweight breathable fabrics. Late night screening means the crowd can dress slightly more stylishly than a daytime match.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: UCL Final screening is football fan fashion at its most passionate — club jerseys are the non-negotiable hero piece. The beauty of the UCL Final is the diversity of club colors on display — Real Madrid white, Barcelona blue-red, Man City sky blue, Bayern red, PSG blue — the screening venue becomes a stadium of global fan fashion. Style the jersey as a fashion piece — men layering the club jersey over a longline white or black tee with cargo shorts or slim joggers in matching team colors. Women tying or cropping the jersey over a co-ord in complementary team colors, or wearing a team-color co-ord with the jersey as a layer. UCL branding colors — navy and gold with star motifs — are a strong neutral option for fans who want to represent the competition rather than a specific club. Caps in club colors or a simple navy snapback. May Bengaluru heat means lightweight polyester jerseys and cotton shorts are the most comfortable combination for a late-night screening. Socks in team colors. Minimal jewellery — a simple watch, a team-color bracelet. Kids club jerseys are highly relevant — UCL Final is a family viewing event. The overall energy is — dressed for the biggest 90 minutes in club football with the pride of a stadium crowd.

app.headline: Champions League Final. Pick Your Colors.
lines: Club jersey out, cap on — UCL Final screening sorted.
White, blue, or red — your club, your kit, your night.
Style your football jersey like the trophy is already yours.

social_media.headline: UCL Final Night. Dress Like A Fan.
lines: POV: styled your club jersey as a fashion piece for the UCL Final screening and the sports bar noticed.
Champions League fit check — club jersey, team colors, navy and gold accents. Biggest night in football starts now.

events.title: IPL 2026 Finals
events.genres: Sports, Cricket, Entertainment, Celebration, National Pride
events.description: IPL 2026 Finals — the grand finale of the world's biggest T20 cricket league, where two franchises battle for the ultimate prize in a packed stadium of roaring fans and millions watching from every corner of India.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: IPL 2026 Finals is the single biggest domestic cricket fashion moment of the year — genZScore 8-9. The Finals energy is significantly more elevated than the season opener — this is the culmination of two months of franchise pride and fan investment. The crowd is maximally passionate, maximally dressed, and maximally loud. Two distinct contexts: (1) stadium attendees — full franchise kit, face paint, scarves, flags, the whole fan experience; (2) watch party and sports bar crowd — elevated jersey styling, smart casual with team colors, more fashion-forward than a regular match. The Finals demand the jersey be styled not just worn — the GenZ crowd goes hardest here. IPL Finals also brings out the most creative fan fashion — customized jerseys, co-ords in team colors, franchise-colored accessories. Caps are the single most important accessory — franchise caps at their most relevant moment of the season. May Bengaluru heat means lightweight fabrics are essential. The Finals night energy means the crowd dresses with the intention of a celebration — because someone is going to win.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: IPL Finals is the most intense cricket fashion moment of the entire season — dress like your team is already lifting the trophy. The franchise jersey is the undisputed hero piece but the Finals demands it be styled with maximum intention. Men in the franchise jersey over a longline tee with cargo shorts or slim joggers — the Finals uniform elevated. Women cropping or tying the jersey over a matching team-color co-ord, or wearing a franchise-color set with the jersey as an outer layer — fashion-forward fan dressing at its best. Color-blocking in franchise palettes is a strong catalog direction for the Finals — MI blue and gold, CSK yellow and blue, RCB red and black, KKR purple and gold, GT blue and gold — all catalog-matchable combinations. Caps are at their absolute most relevant tonight — franchise caps with logo detail or a solid team-color snapback are the single most important accessory. Socks in team colors add a fun detail. Sunglasses for stadium attendees in the May heat. May Bengaluru heat means lightweight polyester jerseys and breathable cotton shorts — comfort is critical for a full evening of high-stakes cricket. Kids franchise jerseys and tees are the most shoppable kids category of the season. Watches with sporty straps. The Finals energy means the crowd celebrates before, during, and after — dress for the whole night.

app.headline: IPL Finals. Dress Like You've Already Won.
lines: Franchise jersey, team-color cap, Finals night sorted.
Style your kit like the trophy presentation is the after-party.
One match, two teams, zero basic fits allowed.

social_media.headline: Finals Night. Full Franchise Fashion.
lines: POV: styled your IPL jersey as a complete fashion moment for the Finals and won before the toss.
IPL Finals fit check — franchise jersey layered, team-color cap on, co-ord underneath, zero holding back. Let's go.

events.title: Buddha Purnima
events.description: Buddha Purnima marks the birth, enlightenment, and passing of Gautama Buddha — observed with deep reverence through prayers, meditation, temple visits, lantern offerings, and a profound collective stillness that honors the path of peace.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Buddha Purnima is one of the most spiritually significant days in the Buddhist calendar — genZScore 2-4. The observance is deeply meditative and devotional — temple visits, prayer gatherings, meditation sessions, and lantern ceremonies. The fashion language is the purest and most minimal of any religious festival — white is the sacred color of Buddha Purnima, representing peace, purity, and enlightenment. The entire color brief is white, off-white, and cream — no exceptions. The aesthetic is serene, minimal, and deeply intentional. Simple cotton or linen ethnic and fusion pieces — no embellishment, no heavy embroidery, no party elements. Women in simple white cotton sarees, white salwar suits, or plain white ethnic dresses. Men in white kurta-pyjama or dhoti-kurta. May in Bengaluru is warm — breathable natural fabrics are essential. Jewellery is completely minimal or absent — the day calls for inner focus, not outward display. The overall fashion energy is the most minimal and spiritually grounded of the entire calendar.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Buddha Purnima has one absolute color rule — white only, always. The simplest and purest dressing of the entire calendar. Women in a plain white cotton saree with a minimal border or no border at all — the cleaner the better. A simple white salwar suit with chikankari or block-print detailing in white-on-white is beautifully appropriate. Men in a crisp white kurta-pyjama or dhoti-kurta — khadi fabric is uniquely resonant for the Gandhian simplicity reference that aligns with Buddhist values. May Bengaluru heat makes breathable 100% cotton and linen the only practical fabric choices — comfort is itself a spiritual act today. Western white dressing is also appropriate for the younger crowd — a clean white linen shirt with white trousers or a simple white cotton dress. Jewellery is minimal to absent — if worn, only the most delicate and simple pieces. A thin silver chain, small stud earrings, a plain bangle — nothing that draws attention. Lotus motif accessories are uniquely relevant — the lotus is the sacred symbol of Buddhist enlightenment. Simple canvas or cotton tote bags in white or natural tones. The overall energy is — dressed in the color of peace, as still and intentional as the day itself.

app.headline: Dressed In Peace. Buddha Purnima.
lines: White cotton, minimal jewels, and a still heart — sorted.
The purest color for the most sacred day of stillness.
Simple white kurta or saree — both are offerings today.

social_media.headline: All White. Full Peace. Buddha Purnima.
lines: POV: wore a plain white cotton saree to Buddha Purnima and felt the weight of everything lift.
Buddha Purnima fit check — white only, breathable cotton, minimal jewels, dressed in the color of enlightenment.

events.title: The Yellow Diary Live
events.genres: Music, Live Concert, Alt-Rock, Indie, Pop Rock
events.description: The Yellow Diary brings their soulful alt-rock sound live — an intimate night of poetic Hindi lyrics, soaring guitar, and the kind of emotionally honest music that makes every song feel like a page from your own journal.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: The Yellow Diary is one of India's most beloved indie alt-rock bands — genZScore 6-8. Their audience is emotionally intelligent, aesthetically aware GenZ and millennials who connect deeply with their poetic Hindi songwriting. Roz, Woh Log, Meri Taraf — their catalog is deeply personal and the crowd dresses to match that intimacy. The fashion energy sits between Anuv Jain's warm earth tones and Linkin Park's dark alt energy — indie alt-rock occupies a middle ground that is edgier than folk but warmer than metal. Think vintage-inspired prints, interesting textures, dark earthy tones, band tees styled with intent, and the kind of layered dressing that feels both casual and considered. The yellow color reference from their band name gives a unique fashion angle — a warm mustard or yellow accent piece is deeply on-brand for this specific concert. Colors are warm-dark — olive, rust, mustard, charcoal, brown, burgundy, cream. May in Bengaluru means warmth — lighter fabrics but the layered indie aesthetic still applies. Jewellery is minimal and artisanal.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: The Yellow Diary live is warm indie-alt dressing at its most considered — dress like the songs feel. The band name gives a unique fashion permission — a mustard or yellow piece is the single most on-brand color choice for this specific concert. Women in a mustard or olive linen co-ord, a vintage-inspired floral dress in warm tones, or a rust knit top with wide-leg corduroy trousers. A yellow graphic tee styled with dark denim and a denim jacket is peak Yellow Diary fan dressing. Men in a warm-toned linen or cotton shirt — olive, rust, or mustard — with straight-fit jeans or chinos, sleeves rolled, half-tucked. The band tee is relevant here — a Yellow Diary graphic tee or any indie band tee styled with intention. Layering with a lightweight jacket or overshirt for the May Bengaluru evening. Denim in washed or faded tones is the most relevant bottom. Artisanal minimal jewellery — thin gold chains, small hoop earrings, a simple ring. Canvas tote or small crossbody bag. Vintage-inspired glasses. Caps in neutral tones. The overall energy is — warm, layered, and dressed like Roz is already playing in your head.

app.headline: Roz Plays Better In This Fit.
lines: Mustard, olive, rust — The Yellow Diary color palette sorted.
Indie alt-rock night calls for warm tones and honest dressing.
Dress like every song is a page from your own journal.

social_media.headline: Woh Log Energy. Yellow Diary Night.
lines: POV: wore a mustard linen co-ord to The Yellow Diary live and the whole concert felt like it was written for you.
Yellow Diary fit check — warm earth tones, denim layers, dainty jewels, dressed like the most poetic version of yourself.

events.title: Mortal Kombat II
events.genres: Action, Fantasy, Adventure, Martial Arts, Fighting
events.description: The champions of Earthrealm return — now joined by the legendary Johnny Cage — in the ultimate no-holds-barred battle against Shao Kahn's dark rule. Flawless victory or total annihilation: the fate of every realm hangs on the outcome of the most brutal tournament ever fought.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Mortal Kombat II is one of the most visually distinctive and character-rich action franchises — genZScore 8-9. The MK fan base is passionate, gaming-culture-native, and dresses with deep character knowledge. The screening crowd spans hardcore gamers, action film enthusiasts, and GenZ streetwear fans who treat the MK aesthetic as a fashion reference. Character fashion references are exceptionally strong and catalog-translatable: (1) Scorpion — the most iconic MK character, yellow and black ninja aesthetic, fire energy, chains; (2) Sub-Zero — ice-cold blue and black, the most visually striking character, deep navy and steel blue; (3) Johnny Cage — the most fashion-forward MK character by far, Hollywood action star energy, sunglasses, tight black tee, signature green shorts, very GenZ streetwear adjacent; (4) Shao Kahn — dark imperial villain, deep maroon, black, and gold; (5) Kitana — royal blue with fan weapons, elegant but deadly. The poster colors — dusty rose-grey and near-black — suggest a more muted editorial approach to the otherwise maximally bold MK palette. May in Bengaluru is intense heat — lightweight breathable fabrics for the screening crowd. The martial arts angle opens a strong athleisure and tactical fashion direction alongside the character-coded streetwear.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Mortal Kombat II screening is the most character-coded gaming fashion moment of May — five iconic fighters give direct catalog direction and the crowd knows exactly which character they're representing. Scorpion direction — the most popular and most catalog-translatable. Yellow and black color-blocking is the brief — a yellow graphic tee with black cargo trousers, or a yellow and black co-ord with chain jewellery. The chain necklace is the single most Scorpion-coded accessory in the catalog. Sub-Zero direction — the most editorial and visually striking option. Deep navy and steel blue with black — a navy co-ord with icy silver accessories, or an all-navy set with metallic chain jewellery. Sub-Zero's ice palette in May's heat is a genuinely refreshing aesthetic choice. Johnny Cage direction — the most GenZ streetwear-adjacent character in MK history. Cage's Hollywood action star energy translates to a fitted black tee, signature green shorts or trousers, and bold gold-framed sunglasses — the most fashion-forward screening look. Shao Kahn direction — dark imperial villain energy. Deep maroon and black with gold accents — a structured maroon blazer with black trousers and gold chain. Kitana direction — royal blue elegant power dressing for women. Bold chain jewellery is the universal MK accessory — every character has chains in some form. Dragon motif pieces are uniquely relevant for the franchise. May heat means lightweight fabrics — polyester and cotton blends in bold colors stay vibrant and breathable.

app.headline: Prepare For The Next Level Of Dressing.
lines: Scorpion yellow, Sub-Zero blue, or Johnny Cage green — pick your fighter, pick your fit.
The most character-coded screening of May needs the most intentional outfit.
Flawless victory goes to whoever wears the chain necklace tonight.

social_media.headline: Finish Him. With This Outfit.
lines: Mortal Kombat II dress code — Scorpion's yellow and black, Sub-Zero's navy and silver, or Johnny Cage's sunglasses-and-attitude. Choose your fighter.
Get over here — and bring the yellow and black co-ord. Mortal Kombat II hits screens May 8th. 🔥🧊
